title: festuca arenaria var. halmyris
intro: Festuca arenaria var. halmyris, also known as Bulgarian fescue, is a type of plant belonging to the grass family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's dried leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
